p.seg: To all sons and faithful of Holy Mother Church to whom the present deed shall come, present and future, William son of Gamel of Twynholm sends Greeting:

I make known to you all that I, with the consent of my lord, Alan son of Roland, and the agreement of Walter my son and heir, have given and granted, and by this my charter made firm, to God and to the church of Holyrood in Edinburgh and to the canons serving God there, in free and pure and perpetual alms, the whole right that I thought good to have in the church of Twynholm, and I have renounced in perpetuity, for myself and my heirs, the claim that I claimed in the advowson of that church. Furthermore I give and grant, with the assent of Walter my son himself, to the foresaid church and the foresaid canons, in perpetual alms, four acres of land in the territory of the same toun, for the canons themselves to hold the same bounds by which I perambulated that very land in the sight of worthy men. And to the foresaid church I give and grant one dwelling in the toun itself, in respect of which I have put the foresaid canons in possession, with common pasture of the same toun for sixteen draught animals and for one horse, and with all other common easements of the same toun. Wherefore I will that the aforenamed canons should hold and possess in perpetuity the foresaid church with all its just pertinents, freely and quietly in perpetual and pure alms.
With these witnesses, Silvester, Andrew and Thomas, chaplains, Matthew the dean, Robert the clerk, Robert Halfknight, Robert and Gamel, brothers, Robert son of Matilda, and very many others.
